title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. loss against Türkiye in the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: United States loses 3-2 to Türkiye in final group stage game of 2026 World Cup.
Auston Trusty scored early for the U.S., but Türkiye responded with goals from Arda Güler and Orkun Kökçü.
Despite loss, U.S. wins Group D and will face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the knockout stage.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com features a news quiz on recent Greater Boston events, testing readers' local news knowledge.
The quiz covers topics such as Mike Wankum's departure and Tall Ship visits to the area.
Peter Chianca has been the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.

title: Moulton allegedly knocks phone from questioner’s hand after being asked about Graham Platner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton allegedly knocked a phone from a questioner's hand at an event in Massachusetts.
Moulton's campaign claims the individual was a political tracker from America Rising, not a Fox News reporter.
Fox News denies paying the individual, stating they never compensate trackers for attending events.

title: Durbin homers to break tie in the Red Sox’s 6-3 win over the Yankees
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Caleb Durbin's two-run homer led the Boston Red Sox to a 6-3 victory over the New York Yankees.
Connelly Early allowed two runs and struck out nine, helping the Red Sox win at Fenway Park for the first time this season.
Yankees starter Cam Schlittler lowered his ERA to 1.62 but left trailing 4-2 due to unearned runs and four errors.

title: 3 injured when car crashes into Newburyport restaurant
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A car crashed into the Black Cow restaurant in Newburyport, injuring three people on Thursday evening.
The driver, a 67-year-old woman from Groveland, was uninjured and cooperating with police; alcohol is not suspected as a factor.
No structural damage was found, but the restaurant's bar area requires repairs; two men were hospitalized, a third declined treatment.

title: Suspect in custody after man, 58, fatally stabbed in Wellesley park
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 58-year-old man was fatally stabbed Thursday night at Phillips Park in Wellesley, according to Norfolk County prosecutors.
James Floyd Bennett III, 26, was arrested and is expected to be arraigned Friday in Dedham District Court.
Prosecutors stated there is no public safety threat following the incident; investigations by State and Wellesley police continue.

title: Framingham police will not renew Flock Safety contract after months of resident opposition
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Framingham police will not renew their contract with Flock Safety after resident advocacy against license plate reader cameras.
The decision follows community concerns over privacy and data sharing, prompting the police to decommission the cameras by June 30.
A proposed ordinance will address use and oversight of license plate reader technology, ensuring data is used only for local enforcement.

title: ‘Over a foot of manure’: Horses, mules among animals rescued from Western Mass. property, MSPCA says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Thirteen animals, including horses and mules, were rescued from unsanitary conditions in Western Massachusetts by the MSPCA-Angell.
Animals lived in over a foot of manure; several were underweight with overgrown hooves, making walking difficult.
Rescued animals are at MSPCA-Angell at Nevins Farm for treatment and adoption; MSPCA seeks more adopters.

title: 10 players Bruins could target in 1st round of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are targeting prospects like Tommy Bleyl and Nikita Klepov in the first round of the 2026 NHL Draft.
Boston Bruins hold eight picks in the 2026 NHL Draft, including the 23rd overall in the first round.
Potential draftees include right-handed defenseman Tommy Bleyl and forward J.P. Hurlbert among others.

title: Coyote warning issued in Beverly after dog attacked on trail
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Beverly police issued a warning after a coyote attack on a dog near the Fox Hill Trail entrance Monday.
A coyote and pups were found in a den 30 feet from the trail entrance, prompting a caution to residents.
Police advise keeping dogs leashed and avoiding coyote dens to prevent incidents, due to their defensive behavior.
